---
title: "Protected Page"
format:
  html: default
  revealjs:
    output-file: protected-page-slides.html
  pdf:
    output-file: protected-page-handout.pdf
  docx:
    output-file: protected-page.docx
---

<div id="password-gate" style="max-width: 500px; margin: 100px auto; padding: 30px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; text-align: center;">
  <h2>🔒 This page is password protected</h2>
  <p>Please enter the password to view this content:</p>
  <form id="password-form" onsubmit="return checkPassword(event)">
    <input
      type="password"
      id="password-input"
      placeholder="Enter password"
      style="padding: 10px; width: 80%; margin: 10px 0; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 4px;"
      autofocus
    />
    <br/>
    <button
      type="submit"
      style="padding: 10px 30px; background-color: #007bff; color: white; border: none; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 16px;"
    >
      Submit
    </button>
  </form>
  <div id="error-message" style="color: red; margin-top: 10px; display: none;">
    Incorrect password. Please try again.
  </div>
</div>

<div id="protected-content" style="display: none;">

# Welcome to the Protected Page {.unnumbered}

Congratulations! You have successfully accessed the password-protected content.

## About This Page

This page demonstrates password protection for a Quarto website. The password is verified client-side using JavaScript, which means:

- It provides basic access control for non-sensitive content
- It's suitable for demos, previews, or educational materials
- It's not secure for truly sensitive information (use server-side authentication for that)

## Protected Content

Here's some example protected content:

### Features Demonstrated

You can include all standard Quarto features on a protected page:

- **Lists** with various formatting
- *Italic* and **bold** text
- `Code snippets` inline
- Links: [Quarto Documentation](https://quarto.org/)

### Code Blocks

You can include code blocks:

```python
# Example Python code on protected page
def secret_function():
    print("This is protected content!")
    return "Secret data"

result = secret_function()
print(result)
```

### Mathematical Equations

You can use LaTeX equations:

$$
E = mc^2
$$

Or inline math like $\pi \approx 3.14159$.

### Callouts

::: {.callout-note}
This is a note callout in the protected content.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Tip for Protected Content
Only users with the correct password can see this tip!
:::

### Theorem-Style Blocks

::: {#thm-protected .thm}
## Protected Theorem
This theorem is only visible to authenticated users.
:::

## Security Note

This is a **client-side** password protection mechanism. The page content is still downloaded to the user's browser; it's just hidden until the correct password is entered. For truly sensitive information, use:

- Server-side authentication
- Content delivery that requires server validation
- A backend service with proper session management

For this demo, the simple client-side approach is sufficient.

</div>

<script>
function checkPassword(event) {
  event.preventDefault();
  const password = document.getElementById('password-input').value;
  const correctPassword = 'passwyrd';

  if (password === correctPassword) {
    // Hide password gate
    document.getElementById('password-gate').style.display = 'none';
    // Show protected content
    document.getElementById('protected-content').style.display = 'block';
    // Store authentication in session storage (persists during browser session)
    sessionStorage.setItem('authenticated', 'true');
    return false;
  } else {
    // Show error message
    document.getElementById('error-message').style.display = 'block';
    // Clear password input
    document.getElementById('password-input').value = '';
    return false;
  }
}

// Check if already authenticated in this session
window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  if (sessionStorage.getItem('authenticated') === 'true') {
    document.getElementById('password-gate').style.display = 'none';
    document.getElementById('protected-content').style.display = 'block';
  }
});
</script>
